commit.message:
fix(model-3d): hide coplanar edges in HEMesh edge rendering

After split-face extrusion, wall faces share coplanar edges with
adjacent side faces. buildHEEdgeGeometry was rendering ALL topology
edges, including these coplanar ones, creating visible diagonal lines
across stair side faces. Now skips edges where both adjacent faces
have matching normals and lie on the same plane.

Also removes diagnostic logging from applyPushPull (kernel is stable).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: 4 issues - NaN hours, unknown commands, reason in admin, A:H reads

- liff-config: normalizeTime() now handles Chinese 下午/上午 AM/PM format
  (e.g. "下午4:00" → "16:00"), fixes workEndTime/workStartTime being wrong
- admin.js: add normTimeInput() to normalize Sheets time before setting
  <input type="time"> values, fixes blank 標準上班時間 display in settings
- employeeService: read A:H, include shiftStart/shiftEnd per employee
  (normalized via normTime); add updateEmployeeShift() to write G:H
- api/admin.js: add update-employee-shift action + import updateEmployeeShift
- app.js v5.10: handleCheckin/handleCheckout prefer employeeData.shiftStart/
  shiftEnd over global liffConfig times, enabling per-employee shift hours
- admin.html/js v3.3: shift edit modal + clock button on each employee card
  so admin can set custom start/end times per employee; shows current shift
  in employee stats row; updates local state after save without full reload

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(core): fix AppTimezone init order and missing import in LongExtensions (#192)

* feat(admin-auth): backend foundation — DB migration, role/permission model, admin config

- V2 Flyway migration: admin_users, admin_sessions, admin_mfa_backup_codes tables
  with brute-force lockout fields (failed_attempts, locked_until) and MFA scaffold
- AdminRole enum (ADMIN, OPERATOR, FINANCE, AUDITOR, HELPDESK)
- AdminPermissions: 37 atomic permissions with role→permission matrix + check() / requirePermission()
- AdminModels: AdminLoginRequest/Response, AdminUserResponse, AdminCreate/UpdateUserRequest
- AppConfig: adminJwtSecret (HS256), adminJwtIssuer, adminAccessTokenTtlMs, adminRefreshTokenTtlDays
- build.gradle.kts: add at.favre.lib:bcrypt:0.10.2 for admin password hashing

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* feat(admin-auth): backend auth service, routes, and DI wiring

- AdminAuthService: bcrypt password verify (at.favre.lib:bcrypt), brute-force lockout
  (5 attempts → 15-min lock), single-use refresh token rotation via SHA-256 hash in DB,
  JWT HS256 issuance/verification, admin user CRUD
- AdminAuthRoutes: POST /admin/auth/{login,refresh,logout}, GET /admin/auth/me,
  GET/POST/PATCH /admin/users, DELETE /admin/users/{id}/sessions
  — httpOnly SameSite=Strict cookies; refresh cookie narrowed to /admin/auth path
- Authentication.kt: added admin-jwt-hs256 named config alongside existing jwt-rs256
- Routing.kt: mount adminAuthRoutes() under auth rate-limit tier
- AppModule.kt: register AdminAuthService(get()) in Koin DI
- V2 migration: timestamps changed to BIGINT epoch-ms (consistent with Exposed long mapping)

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* feat(admin-auth): frontend auth store, api-client, and use-auth replacement

- auth-store.ts: Zustand store for auth state (user, isLoading); not persisted —
  hydrated on every load via /admin/auth/me query
- api/auth.ts: TanStack Query hooks — useCurrentUser() (GET /me), useAdminLogin()
  (POST /login), useAdminLogout() (POST /logout)
- use-auth.ts: replaced CF JWT decoding with auth-store reads; 37-permission matrix
  mirrors AdminPermissions.kt (ADMIN/OPERATOR/FINANCE/AUDITOR/HELPDESK roles)
- api-client.ts: replaced CF-Access-Token header with credentials:'include' (httpOnly
  cookies sent automatically); 401 hook redirects to /login
- constants.ts: removed CF_COOKIE_NAME; added ROUTES.LOGIN
- types/user.ts: aligned AdminRole (ADMIN/OPERATOR/FINANCE/AUDITOR/HELPDESK) and
  AdminUser fields with backend AdminUserResponse schema

- routes/login.tsx: ZyntaPOS branded login page with email+password form;

  errors (401 invalid creds, 429 locked, 403 inactive); redirects to / on success
- routes/__root.tsx: auth guard — calls useCurrentUser() on every load to hydrate
  auth state from httpOnly cookie; redirects unauthenticated users to /login;
  renders AppShell only when authenticated; login page renders without AppShell
- routeTree.gen.ts: manually registered /login route (LoginRoute) in all type

- UserTable: update ROLE_LABELS/ROLE_COLORS to 5-role set, replace

* feat(core): add AppTimezone singleton for system-wide timezone config

- AppTimezone.kt: new singleton in :shared:core holding the active
  IANA timezone (default Asia/Colombo). Exposes set(tzId) for runtime
  updates and current: TimeZone for consumers.
- DateTimeUtils.kt + LongExtensions.kt: all default tz parameters now
  use AppTimezone.current instead of TimeZone.currentSystemDefault(),
  so every date/time display throughout the KMP app automatically
  respects the store-configured timezone.
- ReportRepositoryImpl.kt: tz field changed from val to a computed
  property (get() = AppTimezone.current) so report date-boundary
  queries always use the live configured timezone.
- SettingsViewModel.kt: calls AppTimezone.set() after loadGeneral()
  reads the stored timezone and again immediately after saveGeneral()
  persists a new value — no app restart required.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* feat(admin): timezone store, preferences page, and timezone-aware display

- timezone-store.ts: Zustand store with localStorage persistence
  (key: zynta-admin-prefs). Holds IANA timezone string defaulting
  to Asia/Colombo. Ships a curated TIMEZONE_OPTIONS list of 33 zones.
- utils.ts: add formatEpochDateTime / formatEpochDate / formatEpochRelative
  helpers that accept epoch-ms + IANA tz string and use Intl.DateTimeFormat.
  Also adds tzOffsetLabel() to render "UTC+05:30" offsets in the UI.
- use-timezone.ts: hook returning { timezone, formatDateTime, formatDate,
  formatRelative } — pre-bound to the current store timezone so components
  don't pass the timezone explicitly.
- routes/settings/index.tsx: new Preferences page at /settings with a
  searchable timezone selector (33 options). Saves to Zustand/localStorage
  on "Save Preferences".
- routeTree.gen.ts: register /settings/ route.
- Sidebar.tsx: add "Preferences" nav item (SlidersHorizontal icon) under
  new "Account" group linking to /settings.
- UserTable.tsx: replace inline formatEpoch() with useTimezone().formatRelative
  so Last Login column respects the configured timezone.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

- Move DEFAULT val before _current to fix Kotlin object init order error
- Add missing AppTimezone import in LongExtensions.kt

commit.message:
Initial commit: music library manager with Spotify import and YouTube download

Full-stack app (Express + React) for managing a local music library:
- Spotify integration: browse saved playlists/albums, search Spotify, import metadata
- YouTube download: search videos + playlists, auto-match tracks, batch download with SSE progress
- File organization with album_artist support for compilations (Various Artists)
- ID3 tagging including TPE2 (album artist) for proper Navidrome/Subsonic grouping
- Library browser with artists/albums/songs views
- Post-import full-page download progress view

Add lyrics review session backup/restore system (#509)

## Summary

- Server-side auto-save of lyrics review sessions (every 60s if dirty,
on preview, on page hide) with SHA-256 deduplication
- Split-pane restore UI with session browser, edit diff preview, and
cross-job search
- Auto-prompts on load if saved sessions exist; falls back to
localStorage for local mode

## Changes

### Backend
- **New model**: `ReviewSession` + `ReviewSessionSummary` dataclasses
with Firestore serialization
- **5 new API endpoints** under `/api/review/`: save, list, get, delete
sessions + cross-job search
- **Firestore**: 8 new methods for review session CRUD using
subcollection pattern (`jobs/{jobId}/review_sessions/{sessionId}`)
- **GCS storage**: Correction data stored in GCS (can exceed Firestore's
1MB doc limit); metadata in Firestore
- **Deduplication**: SHA-256 hash comparison skips saving identical data

### Frontend
- **`useReviewSessionAutoSave` hook**: 60s interval auto-save,
visibilitychange save, exposed `saveSession()` for manual/preview
triggers
- **`SessionRestoreDialog`**: Split-pane modal with session list + edit
diff preview, "This Job"/"All Jobs" tabs, cross-job search, duration
mismatch warning
- **`LyricsAnalyzer` integration**: Auto-checks for saved sessions on
load, "Save Progress" and "Session History" toolbar buttons, triggers
save on preview

### Docs
- API.md: 5 new review session endpoints documented
- README.md: Feature added to Current State table + Recent Changes
- LESSONS-LEARNED.md: GCS + Firestore metadata pattern

### Also included (from #508)
- Job observability improvements: timeline metadata, `log_to_job()`
helper

## Testing
- [x] 18 backend unit tests (model serialization, all API endpoints,
deduplication, auth, 404s)
- [x] 10 auto-save hook tests (triggers, deduplication tracking,
interval, read-only, error handling, summary computation)
- [x] 16 SessionRestoreDialog tests (rendering, restore flow, tab
switching, cross-job search, empty states)
- [x] All 607 frontend tests pass
- [x] All 2409 backend tests pass

## Review
- [x] CodeRabbit CLI review completed locally (1 fix applied:
beforeunload → visibilitychange)
- [x] Test review completed (26 new frontend tests added)
- [x] Docs review completed

@coderabbitai ignore

---

feat: reorder protocol phases — PRD before architecture (#22)

* plan: PRD-first protocol phase reorder

Adds implementation plan for reordering the full protocol lifecycle
from discover→plan→solve to discover→define→design→solve, putting
PRD before architecture and splitting them into independent phases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: reorder protocol phases — PRD before architecture

Split the combined `plan` phase into separate `define` (PRD) and
`design` (architecture) phases, enforcing the natural dependency:
requirements must be defined before architecture can be designed.

Key changes:
- full protocol: discover → define → design → solve → implement → review → retro
- feature protocol: define → design → solve → implement → review → retro
- Discovery output renamed from spec.md to discovery-brief.md
- Human gate added to solve phase (story decomposition review)
- New define.yaml and design.yaml checklists (plan.yaml deprecated)
- PM agent reads discovery brief, architect reads approved PRD
- Code reviewer reconciles against PRD instead of spec.md
- discovery-brief.md template added

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: auto-generate changeset for PR #22

* fix: address PR review feedback for protocol phase reorder

- Split regex pipe alternations in discover.yaml into separate checks
- Relax data model checklist check to non-blocking (not all features need one)
- Add interactive_review: true to solve phase in full and feature protocols
- Scope code-reviewer reconciliation to PRD only (discovery brief is analyst's responsibility)
- Fix feature.yaml description to include all phases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove requirements_defined check from discover checklist

The discovery brief is research output, not a specification. Checking for
a "## Requirements" section contradicts the brief-is-research philosophy —
requirements belong in the define phase (PRD), not the discovery brief.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address remaining review feedback — add PRD template, fix stale refs

- Add prd.md template with required sections matching define.yaml gates
- Fix stale "spec" reference in code-reviewer rules
- Clarify multi-faceted-review scope_prd_match is a precondition check
- Reference prd.md template in product-manager agent output
- Update README template count and listing

commit.message:
Fix 5 production bugs: logos, summary truncation, person bg, checkerboard, slide order

1. Logos render ON TOP of gradient overlay (not behind) — can sit anywhere
   on the slide without getting swallowed by the dark fade
2. Summary text allows up to 4 lines to finish sentences; appends "..."
   if still truncated (both sharp and video compositors)
3. Person composite scene prompts now explicitly exclude people from AI
   backgrounds — prevents double-person artifacts (ghost shoulders)
4. Detect fake checkerboard transparency in Google Image results — pixel
   sampling catches gray/white patterns and forces bg-removal
5. All slide DB queries now orderBy(slideIndex) — cover is always first
   in UI, assembly, webhook, and regen routes

execution tasks via MCP — all using existing infrastructure.

Add PB-MGC-Short robustness tests: outlier removal, walk-forward, session, params

Test 1 - Top-trade removal: PF 2.02 → 1.63 → 1.28 → 1.10 after
  removing top 1/2/3 trades. Strategy stays positive but edge thins.
Test 2 - Walk-forward: 2024 solid (PF 1.60), 2025 flat (PF 1.03),
  2026 YTD driven by 2 outlier wins.
Test 3 - Session windows: 09:30-10:30 best (PF 2.22, 19 trades).
Test 4 - Parameter stability: all variations stay PF > 1.5, Sharpe > 2.5.
  No parameter produces negative results. FAST_EMA=8 improves to PF 2.59.

commit.message:
refactor: comprehensive website refactoring

This commit implements a full refactoring of the duyetbot website
addressing multiple issues including light/dark mode support, error
handling, content verification, and code quality improvements.

## Features Added

### Light/Dark Mode Support
- Added CSS variable-based theming with manual toggle
- System preference detection via prefers-color-scheme
- Theme persistence with localStorage
- Warm off-white light palette / dark AI aesthetic

### Build System Improvements
- Added comprehensive error handling with try-except blocks
- Created config.yml for external configuration
- Added frontmatter validation (title, date, description)
- Added date format validation (YYYY-MM-DD)
- Environment variable overrides for config

### Code Quality Improvements
- Fixed hardcoded colors to use CSS variables
- Consolidated duplicate CSS theme definitions
- Added theme constants to JavaScript (reduce stringly-typed code)
- Removed magic theme default from HTML (no flash)

### Content Verification
- Removed fabricated "350TB migration" claim from capabilities.md
- Added missing frontmatter to 2026-02-20.md post

### Documentation Updates
- Updated README with new features and troubleshooting
- Documented light/dark mode support
- Added configuration file documentation

## Technical Details

- CSS: ~60 lines of duplicate theme code eliminated
- Build: Graceful degradation when templates/files missing
- Config: PyYAML optional, falls back to sensible defaults
- Theme: Shared CSS rules for manual toggle + system preference

Mobil va Admin UI yangi ekranlar: Growth, Wallet, RBAC

Mobil ilova (3 yangi ekran):
- ReferralScreen: referral kod, do'stlarni taklif qilish, kampaniyalar
- CashbackScreen: cashback tarixi va statistikasi
- WalletTopUpScreen: hamyon to'ldirish (Click, Payme, Uzum, Apelsin)

Admin panel (3 yangi sahifa):
- Growth: referral trend, kampaniyalar, partner analitikasi
- WalletAdmin: hamyonlar, balanslar, double-entry ledger
- AdminRBAC: 6 rol permission matritsasi, admin boshqaruvi

Navigation va sidebar yangilandi, Figma Blueprint hujjati qo'shildi

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Platform fixes: RLS, likes, Go Live, console.logs, loading states, a11y

- Fix Go Live streaming by integrating LiveKit (was capturing but not transmitting)
- Patch RLS on ai_credit_balances/purchases (remove overly permissive policies)
- Add RLS + optimistic like/unlike for community posts
- Remove ~70 debug console.logs from production code
- Add skeleton loading states to Memory and Palace pages
- Fix Palace floor progress bug (was hardcoded to false)
- Add save-to-library for PTScrabble study transcripts
- Accessibility pass: ARIA labels, roles, aria-expanded on navigation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(combat): fix Time.timeScale=0 blocking Invoke in CombatScene + PlayerPrefs persistence

WorldMapUIController.OpenWindow() sets Time.timeScale=0 which persisted into
CombatScene, preventing Invoke(0.5f) from ever firing (CombatTeamSpawner/EnemySpawner
never spawned units). Fix: reset Time.timeScale=1 before SceneManager.LoadScene.

Add PlayerPrefs-based team persistence to survive domain reload in builds:
- TeamAssemblerData.SaveToPrefs(): serializes team (AnimalData names + stats) and
  selected stage name before scene load
- TeamAssemblerData.LoadFromPrefs(): restores team via Resources.Load<AnimalData>
  in CombatScene if team is empty after domain reload
- StageManager.SetSelectedStage: also writes stage name to TeamAssemblerData
- CombatTeamSpawner.SpawnPlayerTeam: calls LoadFromPrefs if team is empty

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: knowledge lifecycle and match telemetry (#13)

* feat: add match telemetry and knowledge lifecycle management

Add telemetry module that records match events (count, timestamps,
session IDs) per indexed entry. The UserPromptSubmit hook now persists
this data to ~/.claude/cache/skill-router-telemetry.json.

Extend /sleep to manage the full knowledge lifecycle:
- Audit CLAUDE.md for task-specific sections to migrate to skills
- Audit rules/ for situational rules to demote to skills
- Review telemetry to recommend promotions (memory→rule) and
  demotions (rule→skill) based on match frequency
- Consolidate related memories into richer skills

Extend /deep-sleep to classify new learnings into the right type:
- Patterns corrected 3+ times become rules (not just memories)
- Checks existing rules to avoid duplicates

Closes #12

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use skill-router's own semantic search for deduplication

Both /sleep and /deep-sleep now call $PLUGIN_ROOT/bin/skill-router
to check for overlapping entries before creating new ones, instead
of manually listing and reading files. The system should eat its
own dog food.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: change /sleep cadence from weekly to daily

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: change /deep-sleep cadence from weekly to daily

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: log telemetry errors to stderr instead of silently swallowing

Co-authored-by: Jim Park <jim80net@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add daily lifecycle cron setup and project registry

SessionStart now:
- Registers the current project in a known-projects registry
  (~/.claude/cache/skill-router-projects.json)
- Checks if a system cron entry exists for daily /sleep + /deep-sleep
- If missing and lifecycle.enabled, injects context prompting Claude
  to set up the crontab (non-intrusive, scheduled overnight)

New config section: lifecycle.enabled, lifecycle.dailyAt (default
"03:00"), lifecycle.projects (explicit list, defaults to registry).

New bin/lifecycle.sh iterates over projects and invokes claude --print
for each, running /sleep and /deep-sleep headlessly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: rename lifecycle to sleepSchedule

Rename all references from "lifecycle" to "sleepSchedule" /
"sleep-schedule" for consistency with the sleep/deep-sleep naming.

- Config: lifecycle → sleepSchedule
- Type: LifecycleConfig → SleepScheduleConfig
- Script: bin/lifecycle.sh → bin/sleep-schedule.sh
- Cron marker: skill-router-lifecycle → skill-router-sleep

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove stale 3-day watermark timer from cron check

System crontab is permanent — the watermark only needs to track
whether we've already prompted, not re-prompt every 3 days. That
was a remnant of the earlier CronCreate approach.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review findings

- Derive plugin root from import.meta.url instead of hardcoded path
- Use atomic write (temp+rename) in project-registry and watermark
- Only record telemetry for entries that were actually injected,
  not all search results (avoids inflated match counts)
- Remove unused DIR/ROOT vars from sleep-schedule.sh
- Fix stale "lifecycle" references in comments and log messages
- Log exit code on sleep-schedule.sh failures
- Add test for malformed dailyAt fallback to 03:00

Add portrait mobile mode, virtual joystick, fire button, and menu button

Mobile (touch device):
- Portrait mode: 400×700 canvas — natural vertical shooter layout
- Landscape mobile: 800×600 with mobile controls at bottom
- Virtual floating joystick (bottom-left): touch anywhere in left 55% to anchor
  and drag for 8-directional movement
- Fire button (bottom-right): large tap target with glow feedback
- Player movement zone respects control overlay area (PLAYER_Y_MAX)

Navigation:
- ESC key returns to main menu from gameplay or game over
- "◄ MENU" button in HUD (top-right, visible during play)
- "◄ MAIN MENU" button on game over screen
- Orientation change mid-game safely returns to title screen

Title screen:
- Portrait layout: difficulty buttons stack vertically
- Landscape layout: unchanged horizontal row
- Shows mobile-specific controls hint when on touch device

Desktop:
- All existing controls unchanged (WASD, arrows, mouse follow, space to fire)

manifest.json: orientation changed from "landscape" to "any"

commit.message:
feat(lab): configurable --workers for train parallelism

Default worker count is now CPU cores − 1 (clamped to [1, 8]),
leaving one core free for the parent process and system responsiveness.

  uv run rating train                  # auto: CPU cores − 1
  uv run rating train --workers 2      # explicit: 2 workers
  uv run rating train --workers 1      # force sequential
